intro: Rosa pendulina haematodes, also known as the Scotch rose or blood-red wild rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to provide relief from various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Rosa pendulina haematodes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
